hero.title: Designing furniture with clarity, character, and longevity.
hero.body: I'm Jon Brooks, an industrial designer creating considered furniture and objects with quiet silhouettes, honest materials, and details built to age well.

workIntro: A focused selection of furniture studies balancing softened forms, durable joinery, and materials that invite touch.

projects.title: Contour Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Dark olive upholstery, sculpted walnut frame, recessed support rails
projects.summary: A low lounge chair study with a broad upholstered seat held inside a continuous timber frame. The side profile is the main gesture: quiet, reclined, and shaped to feel substantial without looking heavy.
notes: Swept rear frame creates a clear silhouette from the side.
Dark upholstery lets the cushion read as one calm volume.
Low seat angle gives the chair a relaxed lounge posture.

projects.title: Silhouette Sofa
projects.materials: Olive boucle upholstery, hardwood frame, blackened stone table
projects.summary: A low, rounded sofa study designed around a single continuous gesture. The back, arms, and seat read as one softened volume while the foreground table gives the piece a grounded architectural counterpoint.
notes: Continuous wraparound back for a calm profile from every angle.
Dense upholstery texture chosen to hold shadow and soften wear.
Low table pairing reinforces the sofa's horizontal silhouette.

projects.title: Arc Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Cream boucle, sculpted walnut, concealed webbing
projects.summary: A compact chair built around a continuous walnut arm rail. The cream upholstered forms sit inside the frame rather than on top of it, making the construction feel light without becoming fragile.
notes: Rounded walnut transitions remove hard stops at the arm and front leg.
Generous cushion radius gives the chair a softer domestic posture.
Contrasting upholstery and timber make the frame legible at a glance.

projects.title: Foundry Sideboard
projects.materials: Ribbed walnut, satin finish, slim timber legs
projects.summary: A long storage piece with a precise ribbed front and restrained styling surface. The rhythm of the vertical fluting adds depth while keeping the cabinet quiet enough for living spaces.
notes: Vertical ribbing breaks up the broad face without visual noise.
Inset legs keep the cabinet mass floating just above the floor.
Satin walnut finish holds detail in low light and avoids glare.

about.title: About
about.body: Jon Brooks is a furniture and object designer working across seating, storage, and interior objects. The work is shaped by a practical industrial-design background: concept development, material decisions, prototyping, and the small construction details that make a piece feel resolved in daily use.
about.experienceIntro: Past work that supports the furniture practice, from early concept and product development through hands-on prototyping, fabrication, and client presentation.

experience.description: Creating seating, storage, and interior objects with a focus on proportion, material character, presentation imagery, and build-ready design intent.
Developed product concepts, refined forms, compared materials, and prepared visual direction for design reviews and client decisions.
Worked through mockups, joinery decisions, finish samples, and prototype iterations so furniture details could be tested before final build.
Translated room context, use requirements, and material preferences into clear furniture directions for bespoke and small-batch pieces.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For commissions, collaborations, and project inquiries, send a note with the scope, timeline, room context, and materials you have in mind.
